incidents.summary: The first cases of H5N1 bird flu have been detected in Australian seabirds. A confirmed case was found in a brown skua in Cape Le Grand, Western Australia, and a suspected case in a giant petrel near Hawks Nest, New South Wales. Additionally, CSIRO lab testing confirmed the strain in a greater crested tern.

news.headline: A suspected bird flu case has now been found in NSW. Here’s what that means

logs.summary: The first cases of H5N1 bird flu have been detected in Australian seabirds. A confirmed case was found in a brown skua in Cape Le Grand, Western Australia, and a suspected case in a giant petrel near Hawks Nest, New South Wales. Additionally, CSIRO lab testing confirmed the strain in a greater crested tern.

incidents.summary: On November 19, 2025, Mount Semeru in Indonesia erupted, sending ash more than 6,500 feet into the sky and threatening air traffic. The eruption prompted the rescue of over 170 stranded climbers, with authorities evacuating more than 900 people in total.

logs.summary: On November 19, 2025, Mount Semeru in Indonesia erupted, sending ash more than 6,500 feet into the sky and threatening air traffic. The eruption prompted the rescue of over 170 stranded climbers, with authorities evacuating more than 900 people in total.

news.headline: Indonesia evacuates stranded climbers after Semeru volcano erupts | Honolulu Star-Advertiser
news.body: JAKARTA >> Indonesian authorities evacuated more than 900 people and were facilitating the safe return on Thursday of 170 climbers stranded after the eruption of its Semeru volcano, one of the country’s tallest mountains.

news.headline: Volcano eruption forces the rescue of more than 170 climbers on Indonesia's Mount Semeru
news.body: Indonesian authorities say they are helping more than 170 climbers caught by Mount Semeru's sudden eruption to return to safety.

news.headline: Volcano erupts, sending ash over 6,500 feet into the sky, threatening air traffic in Indonesia
news.body: Indonesia’s Mount Semeru volcano erupted around 4:00 p.m. local time, sending ash more than 6,500 feet into the air, according to data from the country’s Center for Volcanology.

incidents.summary: Severe drought and heat across Europe are shrinking harvests, notably French maize, and causing water shortages in the Netherlands. The Rhine River has hit its lowest July level for decades, threatening energy and logistics, while Greek islands face drought during the tourist season; economists warn this extreme heat could raise food prices next year.

incidents.summary: A powerful earthquake with a magnitude between 7.4 and 7.7 struck off the northeast coast of Japan on April 20, 2026, prompting a short-lived tsunami warning for coastal areas. The Japan Meteorological Agency issued alerts, and local residents were advised to evacuate to higher ground. Additionally, misinformation about an "artificial earthquake" began spreading online following the event.

logs.summary: A deadly 7.8 magnitude earthquake struck off the coast of the southern Philippines on June 8, 2026, killing several dozen people and collapsing structures, including in General Santos. Reports also note that an older earthquake video resurfaced online after the tremor. Schools cancelled classes as officials responded to the disaster.

incidents.summary: Wildfires continue to rage in Portugal, Spain, and Greece following a June heatwave that caused thousands of excess deaths. Hundreds of firefighters are battling the blazes, and Portugal is exploring a shift from fire suppression to prevention by improving forest management, described as a "fire paradox."
